sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 66:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 66:1
items.html: Because there was already illumination of Hochma in phase three, she did not have to perform a new intensification for the light of Hochma itself because the illumination of Hochma alone is quite sufficient for her sustenance. This is why this extension is regarded as possible, meaning it was possible to become separated from it. She is regarded as intending because she lacked that light of Hochma itself that she drew, and she had a great yearning when she drew it.
